zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas Maddox Lord of Llanfrynach於1550年出生在威爾士布雷康史爾時，他的父親John Powel “Lord of Maermawr” Madog25歲，母親Mallt Fwyall Howell22歲。他於1570年12月8日與Margarette Morgan在英國倫敦結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於1639年5月7日在英國爾切斯特享年89歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Thomas Maddox Lord of Llanfrynach于1550年出生在威尔士布雷康史爾时，他的父亲John Powel “Lord of Maermawr” Madog25岁，母亲Mallt Fwyall Howell22岁。他于1570年12月8日与Margarette Morgan在英国倫敦结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。他去世于1639年5月7日在英国爾切斯特享年89岁。

nameMeanings.name: Maddox
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Welsh origin): from the Welsh personal name Madog (see Maddock ). Variant of Maddocks .

variants.name: Mattix
meanings: English (of Welsh origin): variant of   Maddox  .

variants.name: Maddock
meanings: English (of Welsh origin): from the Welsh personal name Madawc, Madog, from the ancestor of Welsh madog ‘goodly’. As a personal name and surname it was quite common in medieval Wales, in English counties bordering Wales, and in late medieval West Cornwall.

variants.name: Maddix
meanings: English (of Welsh origin): variant of   Maddox  .

meanings: English (of Welsh origin): variant of   Maddox  .

variants.name: Maddux
meanings: English (of Welsh origin): variant of   Maddox  .

variants.name: Mattocks
meanings: English (of Welsh origin): variant of   Maddox  .

variants.name: Mattox
meanings: English (of Welsh origin): variant of   Maddox  .

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Maddox Lord of Llanfrynach was born in 1550, in Breconshire, Wales, his father, John Powel “Lord of Maermawr” Madog, was 25 and his mother, Mallt Fwyall Howell, was 22. He married Margarette Morgan on 8 December 1570, in London, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died on 7 May 1639, in Chester, Cheshire, England, at the age of 89.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas Maddox Lord of Llanfrynach est né en 1550, à Breconshire, Galles, son père, John Powel “Lord of Maermawr” Madog, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Mallt Fwyall Howell, avait 22 ans. Il a épousé Margarette Morgan le 8 décembre 1570, à Londres, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Il est décédé le 7 mai 1639, à Chester, Cheshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 89 ans.

Er hat am 8. Dezember 1570 in London, England Margarette Morgan geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb am 7. Mai 1639 im Alter von 89 Jahren in Chester, Cheshire, England.

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Maddox Lord of Llanfrynach(이)가 1550년 웨일스 브레콘실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Powel “Lord of Maermawr” Madog(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Mallt Fwyall Howell(은)는 22세였다. 그는 1570년 12월 8일 영국 런던에서 Margarette Morgan(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1639년 5월 7일 영국 체실르 체스터에서 89세의 나이로 사망했다.

Он женился на женщине по имени Margarette Morgan 8 декабря 1570 г. в местности Лондон, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он умер 7 мая 1639 г. в местности честир, чешир, Англия в возрасте 89 лет.
